title: Email notifications arriving hours late
body: Task assignment notifications used to arrive within a minute or two. For the past week, they've been arriving 2-4 hours late. This is causing people to miss deadlines because they don't know they've been assigned a task until hours later. It seems to be worse in the morning — notifications sent around 9-10am take the longest to arrive. Afternoon notifications are usually faster but still delayed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report — the time-of-day pattern is a really useful clue.

To help us narrow down where the delay is happening: when a notification finally arrives late, have you noticed whether the email timestamp (the "Date" header or send time shown in your mail client) is close to when you actually receive it, or whether the email itself was *sent* hours late? In other words, does it look like our system is slow to send, or like the email is sitting in transit somewhere?

Also, is this affecting just email notifications, or are you seeing delays in other notification channels too (e.g., in-app notifications, Slack integration, etc.)?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Hmm, I hadn't thought to check the email headers closely, but now that you mention it, I think the send time in the email is also late — like, it's not sitting in my inbox unseen, it genuinely arrives late. So it seems like the system is slow to send rather than a transit issue.

As for other channels, I mostly rely on email so I'm not sure about Slack. I do check the TaskFlow dashboard sometimes and the assignments show up there right away, so the task itself is created on time — it's just the email notification that's delayed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — knowing the dashboard reflects the assignment immediately but the email lags behind tells us the delay is specifically in the notification pipeline, not in task creation.

To help us figure out if this is a system-wide issue or something more targeted: is this happening to everyone on your team, or have you heard from specific people? And does it seem to happen for all types of task assignments (e.g., any project, any assigner), or have you noticed it more with certain projects or when tasks are assigned in bulk?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It's definitely not just me — I've heard complaints from a few other people on my team too. I think it's pretty much everyone, at least in my department. As for the type of assignment, I haven't noticed it being tied to a specific project or assigner. It seems to happen across the board. I don't think we do a lot of bulk assignments though, it's usually one-off task assignments that are delayed. The weird thing is really the timing — morning is consistently the worst.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really useful context — a department-wide issue concentrated in the morning strongly suggests something systemic rather than account-specific.

One thing that could really help us zero in on the cause: you mentioned this started about a week ago. Do you happen to recall if anything changed around that time — a new feature rollout, a change in how your team uses TaskFlow (e.g., a new integration, a shift in working hours, or a big increase in the number of tasks being created), or anything else that coincided with when the delays began?

Also, just to pressure-test the morning pattern: have you or your teammates ever tried assigning a task early in the morning (say before 8am) to see if that's also delayed, or does it really kick in around 9-10am when everyone starts working?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Now that you mention it, I think there was some kind of new feature around that time — like a daily digest or summary email thing? I started getting these marketing-style roundup emails in the morning, maybe around the same time the delays started. I didn't connect the two until you asked, but the timing does line up. I'm not 100% sure exactly when it rolled out though.

As for testing before 8am — I haven't tried that specifically, but I do recall one time I assigned something to someone around 8:30 and they said they got it pretty quickly. It really does seem like the delays kick in right around 9am or so.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's a really interesting connection — the timing of that daily digest feature lining up with when the delays started is a strong lead.

Do you happen to know roughly what time those digest/summary emails arrive in your inbox each morning? If they land around 9am, that would strongly suggest the digest email job is flooding the queue and pushing your assignment notifications to the back of the line.

Also, as a quick sanity check: if you or a teammate assigned a task on a weekend (or outside normal hours), did the notification come through quickly? That would help confirm whether the delay is really tied to peak-hours load versus something else.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, I think the digest emails do arrive around 9am — that's when I usually see them in my inbox. And actually, now that I think about it, I don't think anyone's assigned tasks over the weekend recently, so I can't say for sure about that. But I will say that by the afternoon things seem mostly fine, so it does feel like something is clogging things up in the morning and then it clears out later in the day.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Email notifications delayed 2-4 hours during morning peak, likely caused by daily digest feature saturating the email queue","problem":"Task assignment email notifications that previously arrived within 1-2 minutes are now delayed 2-4 hours. The delay is concentrated in the morning (9-10am) and clears by afternoon. The issue is department-wide, started approximately one week ago, and is causing team members to miss deadlines because they don't learn about assignments promptly. Task creation itself is unaffected — assignments appear on the dashboard immediately.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A daily digest/summary email feature was rolled out approximately one week ago and appears to send around 9am. This digest job is likely flooding the email notification queue at the same time the workday begins and task assignments spike, pushing transactional assignment notifications to the back of the queue. The queue drains over the course of the morning, which explains why afternoon notifications are faster and why an 8:30am assignment was delivered quickly (before the digest job fires).","reproduction_steps":["Confirm the daily digest email job schedule (expected: ~9am)","Assign a task to a user at 8:30am and note email delivery time (expected: fast, within minutes)","Assign a task to a user at 9:15am and note email delivery time (expected: 2-4 hour delay)","Check the email/notification queue depth over the course of a morning to observe the backlog building after 9am and draining by afternoon"],"environment":"Production TaskFlow instance, affects at least one full department. Issue started approximately one week ago, coinciding with the rollout of a daily digest/summary email feature. All projects and assigners affected equally.","severity":"high","impact":"Department-wide. Team members are missing task deadlines because assignment notifications arrive hours late. The dashboard provides a partial workaround (assignments are visible there immediately), but the team's workflow depends on email notifications.","recommended_fix":"Investigate the email notification queue architecture. Likely fixes: (1) Separate the queue for transactional notifications (task assignments) from bulk/batch emails (daily digests) so digest sends cannot starve real-time notifications. (2) Prioritize transactional emails over digest emails in the queue. (3) Rate-limit or stagger the digest job so it doesn't dump all emails at once. (4) Consider sending digests via a separate email sending pathway entirely.","proposed_test_case":"After applying the fix, send a batch of digest emails and simultaneously trigger a task assignment notification. Verify the assignment notification is delivered within 2 minutes regardless of digest queue depth. Run this test at 9am to match peak conditions.","information_gaps":["Exact queue implementation (single queue vs. prioritized, shared vs. separate for digest and transactional)","Precise rollout date of the daily digest feature to correlate with when delays began","Whether weekend or off-hours assignments are also delayed (reporter had no recent data)","Whether other notification channels (Slack integration) are also affected or only email"]}
